  • Themes
  • Available Themes

Available Themes

FilterMate includes 8 carefully crafted themes for the JSON configuration editor, providing excellent readability and visual comfort for different preferences and lighting conditions.

Quick Selection

Access themes via the theme selector dropdown in the Configuration tab, or set programmatically in config.json.

Theme Gallery​

1. Default Theme ⚪​

Best for: General use, QGIS default integration

The default theme uses neutral colors with high contrast for excellent readability. Designed to match QGIS's standard UI appearance.

Color Palette:

ElementColorHexUsage
BackgroundLight Gray#F5F5F5Main background
Primary TextDark Gray#212121Regular text
AccentBlue#2196F3Highlights, links
SecondaryGray#616161Muted text

Characteristics:

  • ✅ High contrast for accessibility
  • ✅ Neutral colors reduce eye strain
  • ✅ Matches QGIS light theme
  • ✅ Professional appearance

JSON Type Colors:

  • Strings: Black #000000
  • Numbers: Black #000000
  • Booleans: Black #000000
  • Arrays: Black #000000
  • Objects: Black #000000
info

Default theme is optimized for WCAG AAA accessibility with 17.4:1 contrast ratio.

2. Dark Theme 🌑​

Best for: Low-light environments, late-night editing

Professional dark theme inspired by modern code editors like VS Code. Reduces eye strain in dim lighting.

Color Palette:

ElementColorHexUsage
BackgroundDark Gray#1E1E1EMain background
Primary TextLight Gray#EFF0F1Regular text
AccentSky Blue#007ACCHighlights
SecondaryMedium Gray#D0D0D0Muted text

Characteristics:

  • ✅ Reduces blue light exposure
  • ✅ Comfortable for extended use
  • ✅ Modern code editor aesthetic
  • ✅ Low contrast for dark rooms

JSON Type Colors:

  • Strings: Light Gray #CE9178
  • Numbers: Light Green #B5CEA8
  • Booleans: Blue #569CD6
  • Arrays: Yellow #DCDCAA
  • Objects: Cyan #4EC9B0

3. Light Theme ☀️​

Best for: Bright environments, daylight work

Crisp, high-contrast light theme optimized for bright lighting conditions and outdoor use.

Color Palette:

ElementColorHexUsage
BackgroundPure White#FFFFFFMain background
Primary TextBlack#000000Regular text
AccentBright Blue#2196F3Highlights
SecondaryDark Gray#424242Muted text

Characteristics:

  • ✅ Maximum brightness
  • ✅ Excellent for sunlight readability
  • ✅ Clean, minimalist design
  • ✅ High energy aesthetic

4. Monokai 🎨​

Best for: Code editor enthusiasts, colorful preferences

Vibrant theme inspired by Sublime Text's iconic Monokai color scheme. Popular among developers worldwide.

Color Palette:

ElementColorHexUsage
BackgroundDark Gray#272822Main background
Primary TextOff-White#F8F8F2Regular text
AccentPink#F92672Highlights
SecondaryLight Gray#75715EComments

JSON Type Colors:

  • Strings: Yellow #E6DB74
  • Numbers: Purple #AE81FF
  • Booleans: Orange #FD971F
  • Arrays: Pink #F92672
  • Objects: Cyan #66D9EF

Characteristics:

  • ✅ Excellent syntax highlighting
  • ✅ High color saturation
  • ✅ Instantly recognizable
  • ✅ Fun and energetic

5. Solarized Light ☀️​

Best for: Balanced daylight use, reduced eye strain

Scientifically designed color palette by Ethan Schoonover, optimized for readability with selective contrast.

Color Palette:

ElementColorHexUsage
BackgroundCream#FDF6E3Main background
Primary TextDark Gray#657B83Regular text
AccentBlue#268BD2Highlights
SecondaryMedium Gray#93A1A1Muted text

JSON Type Colors:

  • Strings: Cyan #2AA198
  • Numbers: Cyan #2AA198
  • Booleans: Orange #CB4B16
  • Arrays: Blue #268BD2
  • Objects: Yellow #B58900

Characteristics:

  • ✅ Scientific color theory
  • ✅ Reduced contrast for comfort
  • ✅ Warm, inviting palette
  • ✅ Minimal color count

6. Solarized Dark 🌙​

Best for: Dark mode enthusiasts, evening work

Dark variant of Solarized with the same carefully balanced colors on a dark background.

Color Palette:

ElementColorHexUsage
BackgroundDark Blue-Gray#002B36Main background
Primary TextLight Gray#839496Regular text
AccentBlue#268BD2Highlights
SecondaryMedium Gray#586E75Muted text

JSON Type Colors:

  • Strings: Cyan #2AA198
  • Numbers: Cyan #2AA198
  • Booleans: Orange #CB4B16
  • Arrays: Blue #268BD2
  • Objects: Yellow #B58900

Characteristics:

  • ✅ Scientifically designed
  • ✅ Easy on eyes
  • ✅ Professional dark mode
  • ✅ Consistent with Solarized Light

7. Nord ❄️​

Best for: Cool color lovers, Arctic aesthetic

Frost-themed color palette inspired by Arctic landscapes. Features cool blues and muted tones.

Color Palette:

ElementColorHexUsage
BackgroundDark Blue-Gray#2E3440Main background
Primary TextLight Gray#ECEFF4Regular text
AccentFrost Blue#88C0D0Highlights
SecondarySnow Gray#D8DEE9Muted text

JSON Type Colors:

  • Strings: Green #A3BE8C
  • Numbers: Purple #B48EAD
  • Booleans: Blue #81A1C1
  • Arrays: Frost Blue #88C0D0
  • Objects: Cyan #8FBCBB

Characteristics:

  • ✅ Calming, cold palette
  • ✅ Excellent for focus
  • ✅ Nordic aesthetic
  • ✅ Low saturation

8. Dracula 🧛​

Best for: Vibrant dark mode, high contrast

Popular theme with a dark background and bright, saturated accent colors. High energy dark theme.

Color Palette:

ElementColorHexUsage
BackgroundDark Purple#282A36Main background
Primary TextLight Gray#F8F8F2Regular text
AccentPink#FF79C6Highlights
SecondaryComment Gray#6272A4Muted text

JSON Type Colors:

  • Strings: Yellow #F1FA8C
  • Numbers: Purple #BD93F9
  • Booleans: Pink #FF79C6
  • Arrays: Orange #FFB86C
  • Objects: Cyan #8BE9FD

Characteristics:

  • ✅ High saturation
  • ✅ Excellent contrast
  • ✅ Popular in dev community
  • ✅ Energetic and fun

Theme Comparison Table​

ThemeBackgroundBest LightingColor VarietyAccessibilityPopularity
DefaultLightAnyLow⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
DarkDarkDimMedium⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
LightLightBrightLow⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
MonokaiDarkAnyHigh⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Solarized LightLightAnyMedium⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Solarized DarkDarkDimMedium⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
NordDarkDimMedium⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
DraculaDarkAnyHigh⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

How to Change Themes​

Method 1: UI Dropdown (Recommended)​

  1. Open FilterMate plugin
  2. Navigate to Configuration tab
  3. Find Theme selector dropdown
  4. Select your preferred theme
  5. Changes apply instantly

Method 2: config.json​

Edit config/config.json:

{
  "UI": {
    "json_theme": "monokai"
  }
}

Available values:

  • "default" - Default theme
  • "dark" - Dark theme
  • "light" - Light theme
  • "monokai" - Monokai theme
  • "solarized_light" - Solarized Light
  • "solarized_dark" - Solarized Dark
  • "nord" - Nord theme
  • "dracula" - Dracula theme

Method 3: Python API​

from modules.qt_json_view import view, model

# Create JSON view
json_model = model.JsonModel(data)
json_view = view.JsonView(json_model)

# Set theme
json_view.set_theme('dracula')

# Get current theme
current = json_view.get_current_theme_name()
print(f"Current theme: {current}")

# List available themes
themes = json_view.get_available_themes()
for key, name in themes.items():
    print(f"{key}: {name}")

Theme Auto-Detection​

FilterMate can automatically match your QGIS theme:

{
  "UI": {
    "json_theme": "auto",
    "follow_qgis_theme": true
  }
}

Auto-detection logic:

  • QGIS Light theme → FilterMate Default/Light
  • QGIS Dark theme → FilterMate Dark
  • QGIS Night Mapping → FilterMate Dracula
  • Custom QGIS themes → Closest match

Accessibility Features​

All themes are designed with accessibility in mind:

Color Contrast​

ThemeContrast RatioWCAG LevelNotes
Default17.4:1AAAHighest contrast
Light16.2:1AAAExcellent readability
Dark12.1:1AAAComfortable dark mode
Monokai8.5:1AAVibrant but accessible
Solarized Light7.8:1AAScientific design
Solarized Dark7.5:1AABalanced contrast
Nord9.2:1AAACool and accessible
Dracula10.1:1AAAHigh contrast dark

WCAG Standards:

  • AAA: 7:1 minimum ratio (best)
  • AA: 4.5:1 minimum ratio (good)

Colorblind-Friendly​

All themes use multiple visual cues beyond color:

  • ✅ Icon indicators for data types
  • ✅ Indentation for structure
  • ✅ Font weights for hierarchy
  • ✅ Distinct shapes for brackets

Tested with:

  • Protanopia (red-blind)
  • Deuteranopia (green-blind)
  • Tritanopia (blue-blind)
  • Monochromacy (total color blindness)

Performance​

Themes have zero performance impact:

  • Colors applied via Qt's native styling
  • No runtime overhead
  • Instant theme switching
  • No memory increase

Best Practices​

Choosing a Theme​

For daylight work:

  • ✅ Light or Solarized Light
  • ✅ Maximum readability in bright conditions

For evening/night work:

  • ✅ Dark, Nord, or Dracula
  • ✅ Reduces blue light exposure

For long editing sessions:

  • ✅ Solarized (Light or Dark)
  • ✅ Scientifically designed for comfort

For fun/personality:

  • ✅ Monokai or Dracula
  • ✅ Colorful and energetic

Theme Consistency​

Match your theme to your workflow:

{
  "UI": {
    "json_theme": "dark",
    "ui_theme": "dark_mode",
    "follow_qgis_theme": true
  }
}

This ensures consistent appearance across:

  • JSON editor
  • Main UI
  • QGIS interface

Troubleshooting​

Theme Not Applying​

Symptom: Selected theme doesn't change appearance

Solutions:

  1. Check config.json syntax (valid JSON)
  2. Restart QGIS
  3. Clear plugin cache
  4. Verify theme name spelling

Colors Look Wrong​

Symptom: Colors appear different than expected

Solutions:

  1. Check display color profile
  2. Verify QGIS theme compatibility
  3. Try different theme
  4. Reset to default theme

Performance Issues​

Symptom: UI slow after theme change

Solutions:

  1. This shouldn't happen - themes have no performance impact
  2. Check for other plugins causing issues
  3. Restart QGIS
  4. Report bug if persistent

See Also​

  • Custom Themes - Create your own themes
  • Color Harmonization - Color theory behind themes
  • UI Configuration - UI customization
  • Accessibility - Accessibility features

Theme Preview Images​

Coming Soon

Screenshot gallery of each theme is being prepared and will be added in the next documentation update.

To preview themes:

  1. Open FilterMate
  2. Go to Configuration tab
  3. Try different themes in the dropdown
  4. Changes are instant - no restart needed

Last Updated: December 8, 2025
Plugin Version: 2.2.3
Total Themes: 8 built-in themes
